description:
You are a platform-engineer tasked with preparing a code-review patch for a CI/CD
    configuration change.

    1.  Two YAML files already exist:
        • /home/user/pipeline_configs/old/config.yml  
        • /home/user/pipeline_configs/new/config.yml

    2.  Produce a unified diff (the standard “-u” format created by the diff utility)
        that shows **only** the differences between the two files and write it to

            /home/user/ci_cd_patch/ci_config.patch

        Required formatting rules that the automated tests will check:
        a.  The very first two header lines of the patch **must** begin exactly with  
            “--- old/config.yml” and “+++ new/config.yml” (note the single space that
            follows the triple dashes).  
        b.  The patch **must** be in unified format (the file will therefore contain
            lines beginning with “@@”, “-”, “+”, and a single leading space for context).  
        c.  No absolute paths should appear inside the patch; only the relative
            paths shown above must be present.  
        d.  The file must end with a single trailing newline character.

    3.  After the patch file has been created, append to a log file named

            /home/user/ci_cd_patch/generation.log

        exactly one line containing the single token

            PATCH_CREATED

        followed by a newline.  Do **not** add any other text to this log file.

    4.  All new artefacts must be placed inside /home/user/ci_cd_patch.  If that
        directory does not yet exist, create it first with default user-level
        permissions.

    A quick recap of the acceptance criteria
    ----------------------------------------
    • /home/user/ci_cd_patch/ci_config.patch exists, is in unified diff
      format, and starts with the required “---” and “+++” header lines.  
    • /home/user/ci_cd_patch/generation.log exists and contains exactly one
      line: “PATCH_CREATED”.  
    • No additional output files are generated.

    You may use whatever shell commands you like (diff, mkdir, echo, etc.),
    but accomplish the whole goal with as few commands as you deem appropriate.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data used by the evaluation harness.
    These values are NOT visible to the agent.
    -->

    <!-- Pre-existing directory structure and file contents -->
    <preexisting>
        <directory path="/home/user/pipeline_configs/old"/>
        <directory path="/home/user/pipeline_configs/new"/>

        <file path="/home/user/pipeline_configs/old/config.yml"><![CDATA[
version: "1.0"
stages:
  - build
  - test
  - deploy
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/pipeline_configs/new/config.yml"><![CDATA[
version: "1.1"
stages:
  - build
  - test
  - deploy
notifications:
  email: devops@example.com
]]></file>
    </preexisting>

    <!-- Files that must be produced by the agent -->
    <expected>
        <directory path="/home/user/ci_cd_patch"/>

        <file path="/home/user/ci_cd_patch/ci_config.patch"><![CDATA[
--- old/config.yml    2024-06-05 12:00:00.000000000 +0000
+++ new/config.yml    2024-06-05 12:00:00.000000000 +0000
@@ -1,5 +1,7 @@
-version: "1.0"
+version: "1.1"
 stages:
   - build
   - test
   - deploy
+notifications:
+  email: devops@example.com
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/ci_cd_patch/generation.log"><![CDATA[
PATCH_CREATED
]]></file>
    </expected>
name: task_000000_5735d4fb
